title: x-lifecycle
description: OpenAPI info-level extension that declares the lifecycle stage, stability, and sunset plan of an API. Makes lifecycle health visible to consumers and AI agents without reading documentation.

stage.description: Current lifecycle stage. Consumers and agents use this to decide whether to build on this API. 'design': not ready for use. 'beta': interface may change. 'stable': safe to build on. 'deprecated': will be removed, use migration-guide. 'sunset': no longer available.

stability.description: Intent and schema stability signal. 'experimental': x-capability.intent may change without notice. 'stable': intent changes are versioned and communicated. 'frozen': no further changes — sunset is planned.

since.description: ISO 8601 date when the current stage began. Consumers can calculate API age and stability duration.

sunset-date.description: ISO 8601 date when this API will stop responding. Required when stage is 'deprecated' or 'sunset'. Consumers should use this to plan migration timelines.

migration-guide.description: URL to the migration guide for consumers moving away from this API. Required when stage is 'deprecated' or 'sunset'.

intent-version.description: Semantic version of the x-capability intent contract. Bump minor when intent descriptions change non-materially. Bump major when the meaning of an operation changes — this is a breaking change for agents.

replacement.description: operationId or API identifier that replaces this one when deprecated. Agents use this for Capability Redirect — self-healing workflows when an API is sunset.

then.description: sunset-date and migration-guide are required when stage is deprecated or sunset
